_meta.name: haiku-templates-v1
_meta.description: POS template library for grammatically coherent, eerily plausible AI-style English nature haiku. Templates are drawn from analysis of actual haiku structure (Basho, Issa, Buson) and their 2022-2024 AI imitations. Each template is a sequence of (POS_tag, syllable_count) slots summing to 5-7-5. Templates are versioned — reversal requires the same template library used during encoding.
_meta.syllable_sum_check: each template must sum to [5, 7, 5] per line
_meta.note: Template selection is driven by forward digits of the prime key. Within each template, word selection uses the reversed digits — the same structural pattern as the Gloss layer. One prime, two schedules, zero additional key material.

templates.name: imagist-classic
templates.description: Two-image structure. The gold standard of haiku. Image / juxtaposition / image.
templates.example: golden leaves falling / the river asks nothing of / the darkening sky

templates.name: gerund-opening
templates.description: Opens with a gerund — extremely common in AI haiku. Action / observation / stillness.
templates.example: falling through the grey / a single leaf finds the ground / silence after

templates.name: the-noun-stack
templates.description: Pure noun accumulation — no verbs, creates a list-like image. Very haiku, very AI.
templates.example: stone / cold water / morning light / the heron still / broken reed

templates.name: subject-verb-image
templates.description: Simple subject-verb sentence followed by an image. The most prose-like template.
templates.example: the old crow waits / on the broken winter fence / snow falls anyway

templates.name: season-turn
templates.description: Seasonal image / shift / consequence. The classical kigo structure.
templates.example: first autumn wind / the leaves do not know yet / they are already gone

templates.name: prepositional-meditation
templates.description: Preposition-heavy. The 'through / beneath / within' structure beloved of AI haiku generators.
templates.example: through the bare branches / something moves that has no name / into the dark

templates.name: past-participle-opener
templates.description: Opens with a past participle — creates a sense of something already done.
templates.example: forgotten garden / the gate still holds its silence / rust on the latch

templates.name: the-lone-witness
templates.description: Single subject watching or being watched. The solitude template.
templates.example: one crow on the wire / watching the grey afternoon / neither moves

templates.name: negation-structure
templates.description: Contains a negation — 'no', 'not', 'nothing'. Creates absence. Very AI.
templates.example: no wind this morning / the pond holds everything still / nothing is moving

templates.name: time-marker-opening
templates.description: Opens with a time reference. Classic haiku grounding technique.
templates.example: late autumn morning / the dogs have stopped their barking / frost on the window

templates.name: colour-noun-verb
templates.description: Colour adjective / noun / simple verb. The Instagram haiku.
templates.example: pale grey morning light / falls across the empty floor / something remembers

templates.name: the-questions-unanswered
templates.description: Implied question with no answer. Creates unease. AI haiku hallmark.
templates.example: who left this candle / burning in the winter room / the door stands open

templates.name: body-and-world
templates.description: The human body in relation to nature. Embodiment haiku.
templates.example: my breath in cold air / the mountain does not notice / I am also small

templates.name: the-still-life
templates.description: Objects described without movement. The Dutch masters as haiku.
templates.example: cold cup on the sill / a book left open to rain / the afternoon light

templates.name: comparative-turn
templates.description: Uses a comparison, implicit or explicit. Creates depth.
templates.example: quieter than snow / the moment before you speak / I hold my breath still

templates.name: the-remembered
templates.description: Past tense / memory structure. The nostalgia template.
templates.example: you used to say that / the autumn always returns / I forget which year

templates.name: nothing-and-everything
templates.description: The grand abstraction with a concrete anchor. AI haiku overcorrecting.
templates.example: everything is still / the pond holds all the morning / a frog waits below

templates.name: season-word-pivot
templates.description: The kigo pivot — season word / action / consequence.
templates.example: first snow of winter / children have already gone / the yard holds their prints

templates.name: adjective-noun-silence
templates.description: Two adjective-noun pairs with a verb of being or silence.
templates.example: pale morning / cold stone / the river does not speak / it simply runs

templates.name: the-approach
templates.description: Something arriving, coming closer. Anticipation.
templates.example: before the rain comes / the birds stop their singing first / then the silence fills

templates.name: deep-image
templates.description: Two images with no explicit connection. Forces the reader to make the leap.
templates.example: the rusted gate latch / a child's shoe in the garden / evening comes early

templates.name: the-instruction
templates.description: Imperative mode — the haiku that tells you what to do. Rare in classical, common in AI.
templates.example: listen to the rain / let it say what it needs to / then let it be still

templates.name: the-inventory
templates.description: Listing without hierarchy. The haiku as catalogue.
templates.example: snow / an open gate / the smell of the neighbour's fire / nothing moving

templates.name: conditional-haiku
templates.description: If-clause structure. The philosophical AI haiku.
templates.example: if the stone could speak / it would tell us about rain / we already know

templates.name: verb-heavy
templates.description: Multiple verbs, little punctuation. Creates motion.
templates.example: the wind lifts and turns / the leaves follow then let go / the branch remembers

templates.name: the-precise-detail
templates.description: One very specific image. The haiku that earns its moment.
templates.example: the third step creaks / she always avoided it / the house still listens

templates.name: the-pivot-line
templates.description: Line 2 belongs to both line 1 and line 3. The kireji pivot.
templates.example: morning fog / lifting from the river / birds begin their work

templates.name: the-plain-observation
templates.description: No metaphor. Just a thing happening. The hardest kind to write, the easiest to fake.
templates.example: the cat sits in sun / on the kitchen windowsill / its eyes are closing

templates.name: the-already-gone
templates.description: Past perfect or past progressive — the thing was happening and is done.
templates.example: they had been laughing / long before the snow arrived / no one remembers

templates.name: two-nouns-one-verb
templates.description: Subject and object with a single precise verb. Economy.
templates.example: the river / carries / what the mountain / releases / to the sea

templates.name: the-interior-weather
templates.description: External nature / internal state / return to nature. The emotion buried in landscape.
templates.example: cold rain on the roof / I cannot say what I mean / the rain continues

templates.name: the-name-withheld
templates.description: Refers to someone without naming them. Intimacy and distance.
templates.example: she left the window / open the way she always did / I closed it at last

templates.name: existential-pivot
templates.description: Statement of existence / complication / resolution or dissolution.
templates.example: there is a moment / between the last light and dark / that belongs to no one

templates.name: the-verb-of-light
templates.description: Light doing something — shining, falling, breaking. High AI frequency.
templates.example: winter light falling / across the kitchen table / nobody is home

templates.name: the-small-animal
templates.description: A small creature as witness or actor. Basho's frog, basically.
templates.example: the sparrow does not / know that winter is coming / it finds another seed

templates.name: three-nouns
templates.description: Three nouns, one per line, almost. Minimalist. Creates white space.
templates.example: stone / silence / the moon asks nothing

templates.name: the-return
templates.description: Something coming back — seasons, people, birds, light.
templates.example: the geese return south / the same sky but not the same / I have changed also

templates.name: the-transformation
templates.description: Something becoming something else. Change as subject.
templates.example: the puddle becomes / a mirror for the grey sky / then the wind erases

templates.name: the-absence-room
templates.description: A space defined by what is missing from it.
templates.example: the empty garden / holds the shape of what was there / nothing grows in grief

templates.name: the-hour
templates.description: A specific time of day as anchor. Morning and dusk dominate AI haiku.
templates.example: four in the morning / the birds have not started yet / I have not slept

templates.name: double-negation
templates.description: Two negatives creating a strange positive space.
templates.example: not the wind / not the light / but the space between them / where nothing is named

templates.name: the-reader-addressed
templates.description: The haiku speaks to 'you'. Second person. AI haiku overuses this.
templates.example: you will not notice / the moment the light has changed / until it has gone

templates.name: the-sound
templates.description: Acoustic haiku — something heard rather than seen.
templates.example: the sound of the rain / on the different surfaces / each one a small grief

templates.name: the-long-view
templates.description: Geological or historical time scale. The AI haiku that wants to be deep.
templates.example: ten thousand winters / the stone has not moved an inch / we call this patience

templates.name: the-threshold
templates.description: A liminal space — doorway, edge, surface, boundary.
templates.example: at the edge of sleep / something is being decided / the body lets go

templates.name: the-botanical
templates.description: Plant life as subject. The haiku of root and branch.
templates.example: the old plum tree / still blossoms in late winter / we do not deserve it

templates.name: the-self-aware
templates.description: The haiku that knows it is a haiku. Peak AI haiku energy.
templates.example: I tried to write it / the moment the light was there / by then it was gone

templates.name: the-kigo-pivot-modern
templates.description: Modern kigo — not cherry blossoms but wifi, screens, the digital season.
templates.example: the screen goes dark first / then everything else follows / winter of the grid
